name: doc-ecosystem-audit
purpose: Comprehensive diagnostic of the documentation ecosystem across 16 categories in 5 domains — index sync, link integrity, content quality, generation pipelines, and coverage completeness — producing composite health grade A-F, trend tracking, and interactive finding-by-finding walkthrough.
notes: No compatibility frontmatter field (only name + description). SKILL.md is 12471 bytes. Delegates ALL shared logic via _shared/ecosystem-audit/ modules — SKILL.md is thin orchestration shell referencing 5 shared files. State file: .claude/tmp/doc-audit-progress.json. Session log: .claude/tmp/doc-audit-session-{date}.jsonl. History: .claude/state/doc-ecosystem-audit-history.jsonl. Uses TDMS via /add-debt. Data sources include DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md, docs/**/*.md, CLAUDE.md, AI_WORKFLOW.md, .husky/pre-commit, scripts/check-cross-doc-deps.js, doc-optimizer skill — ALL SoNash-specific paths requiring sanitization. No Lineage frontmatter (unlike pr-review). References doc-optimizer skill which must exist for full D4 scoring. 24 total files: 1 SKILL.md + 1 entry-point script (14873 bytes) + 5 checker scripts + 6 lib scripts + 5 checker tests + 4 top-level tests + 1 lib scoring property test = 24. Checker tests live in checkers/__tests__/ subdirectory (nested test layout).

name: skill-ecosystem-audit
purpose: Comprehensive diagnostic of the skill ecosystem across 21 categories in 5 domains — structural compliance, cross-reference integrity, coverage/consistency, staleness/drift, and agent orchestration health — producing composite health grade A-F, trend tracking, and interactive domain-chunked walkthrough.
notes: No compatibility frontmatter field (only name + description). SKILL.md is 13392 bytes — largest of the three doc/skill/health audit SKILL.mds. Delegates ALL shared logic via _shared/ecosystem-audit/ modules — same thin-shell pattern as doc-ecosystem-audit. State file: .claude/tmp/skill-audit-progress.json with extended fields currentDomain + domainsCompleted for domain-chunked processing (unique to skill audit). History: .claude/state/skill-ecosystem-audit-history.jsonl. 21 categories vs 16 (doc) vs 26 (health). Domain 5 checks team config health in settings.json — SoNash-specific. References SKILL_INDEX.md and docs/agent_docs/{FIX_TEMPLATES,CODE_PATTERNS}.md which are SoNash-specific. No Lineage frontmatter. 24 total files: identical structure to doc-ecosystem-audit (1 SKILL.md + 1 entry-point + 5 checkers + 6 lib + 5 checker tests + 4 top-level tests + 1 lib property test). Checker domains map to skill ecosystem concepts not portable without project-specific SKILL_INDEX.md and skill registry.

name: market-research-reports
purpose: Generate comprehensive market research reports (50+ pages) in consulting-firm style with LaTeX formatting, scientific diagrams, multi-framework strategic analysis (Porter's Five Forces, PESTLE, SWOT, TAM/SAM/SOM, BCG Matrix), and deep integration with research-lookup and visual generation skills.
external_refs: xelatex (LaTeX compiler — required for PDF output)
notes: SKILL.md frontmatter has allowed-tools field (Read, Write, Edit, Bash) — unique in this batch. No compatibility field. 9 total files: SKILL.md + structure.md + 3 reference MDs (report_structure_guide.md 23682b, visual_generation_guide.md 37529b, data_analysis_patterns.md 20959b) + FORMATTING_GUIDE.md + market_report_template.tex + market_research.sty + generate_market_visuals.py. CRITICAL: 5 dependency skills (research-lookup, scientific-schematics, generate-image, peer-review, citation-management) are NOT present anywhere in the SoNash .claude/skills/ directory or at the referenced skills/ path (which appears to be a non-.claude-prefixed skills/ dir that does not exist). This skill is BROKEN without those dependencies — it is a standalone coordination skill that cannot function without the external skill ecosystem. The script references use relative paths (skills/research-lookup/...) without .claude/ prefix — suggests these skills may have lived in a separate top-level skills/ directory that has since been removed or not yet created. LaTeX toolchain (xelatex, bibtex) is a heavy external requirement. No REFERENCE.md — structure.md serves the reference role with a different name. Market report outputs go to writing_outputs/ (project-relative, gitignored presumably). Version 1.0 only — youngest skill in this batch (same date as doc/skill ecosystem audits but never updated to 1.1). Not part of the _shared/ecosystem-audit family — completely different domain.

name: health-ecosystem-audit
purpose: Comprehensive diagnostic of the health monitoring ecosystem across 26 categories in 6 domains — checker infrastructure reliability, scoring pipeline integrity, data persistence, consumer integration, coverage/completeness, and mid-session alert system — with live test execution, trend tracking, and interactive walkthrough.
dependencies: comprehensive-ecosystem-audit (optional caller — as parallel agent with --skip-live-tests)
notes: SKILL.md is 8897 bytes — most compact of the 4 ecosystem-audits despite having most categories (26). Has REFERENCE.md (14387 bytes) unlike doc/skill ecosystem audits. REFERENCE.md contains all dashboard templates, schemas, category definitions, benchmarks — SKILL.md is an even thinner shell than doc/skill audits. Has a Warm-Up phase with explicit user confirmation gate (doc/skill audits do not). Has Phase 1b Live Test Execution (npm test) — unique to health audit. 8-phase workflow vs 6-phase in doc/skill audits. Dependency on /comprehensive-ecosystem-audit for parallel orchestration (D#49 reference). Phase 8 Closure includes invocation tracking via scripts/reviews/write-invocation.ts (TypeScript, uses npx tsx) — SoNash-specific invocation registry. State file consumed by /alerts for Test Health category (bidirectional coupling: health-ecosystem-audit writes .claude/state/health-ecosystem-audit-history.jsonl that alerts reads). 21 total files: 6 checkers (no __tests__ subdir within checkers — unlike doc/skill audits), 6 lib files, 6 __tests__ files, SKILL.md, REFERENCE.md, run-health-ecosystem-audit.js = 21. Checker structure differs from doc/skill: health checkers do NOT have their own __tests__ subdirectory — all tests consolidated in scripts/__tests__/. Domain 6 (Mid-Session Alert System) unique to health audit — monitors the alert infrastructure itself.

name: alerts
purpose: Lightweight project/codebase health signal with 18 (limited) or 42 (full) category checks, trend detection, cross-category grouping, suppression management, and interactive alert-by-alert workflow — consumer of the health-ecosystem-audit ecosystem, also called from session-begin.
dependencies: alerts-history.jsonl (.claude/state/ — soft, trends omitted if missing)
health-ecosystem-audit-history.jsonl (.claude/state/ — soft, Test Health category omitted if stale >1d)
notes: Significantly different from ecosystem-audit pattern: SMALLEST skill (3 files total), has REFERENCE.md (15510 bytes), run-alerts.js is the LARGEST single script in this batch (148112 bytes — ~10x size of other entry-point scripts). This asymmetry is notable: script complexity is embedded in the JS rather than distributed across checker modules. Acts as CONSUMER of health-ecosystem-audit (not owner). Called from session-begin with --limited mode — tighter integration with session lifecycle than any other audit skill. Has 7-phase workflow (vs 6 for doc/skill, 8 for health). Unique features: volume spike guard (>2x alert count vs previous run flags ecosystem stress), trend data integrity CL verification before computing trends, suppression reason validation (15-char minimum, must reference category/ticket), delegation protocol for bulk actions, convergence loop in Phase 5 fix verification. Cross-boundary with session-begin: uses lastCleared timestamp in hook-warnings-ack.json as shared state boundary. Categories split: 18 limited + 24 additional full = 42 total. Version 3.1 (2026-03-24) — most actively maintained skill in this batch by version number. No active_scripts beyond run-alerts.js itself. State files are more numerous and complex than other audits due to suppression management system.
